item.title: Why Prince Harry isn't staying at Buckingham Palace during U.K. visit
item.description: Prince Harry has returned to the U.K. for the Invictus Games, but he will not be staying at Buckingham Palace during his week-long visit after the Palace says he missed an invitation deadline. CBS News royal contributor Roya Nikkhah breaks down the dispute.

item.title: Tanker struck as Iran threatens to abandon talks over Trump's threats
item.description: A tanker was struck by a projectile in the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Iran continued exchanging threats with peace talks on hold.

item.title: NATO summit in Turkey begins as Trump pushes for more defense spending
item.description: Amid simmering tensions between the U.S. and some NATO allies over Iran and Greenland, President Trump will continue to press other countries to boost defense spending.

item.title: French right-wing leader Marine Le Pen cleared to run in 2027 election
item.description: Le Pen is now eligible to run in the forthcoming election, but would have to do so wearing an electronic tag, something she has previously ruled out.

item.title: Prosecutors present case against man accused of killing Charlie Kirk
item.description: Prosecutors in Utah began presenting their case on Monday against Tyler Robinson, who is accused of killing Charlie Kirk, during a five-day preliminary hearing. Prosecutors are aiming to prove they have enough evidence to move forward with a murder trial against Robinson, who has not yet entered a plea. Carter Evans reports.

item.title: Democrats call on Graham Platner to drop out of Maine Senate race after sexual assault claim
item.description: Graham Platner, the Maine Democratic nominee for U.S. Senate, is facing a sexual assault allegation from a woman he once dated. Platner has denied the claim. At least 13 Democrats have called for him to drop out of the race. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Toyota, in $3.6 billion move, shifting Tacoma production to Texas from Mexico
item.description: Toyota is shifting most production of its midsize Tacoma pickup truck from Mexico to the U.S. as part of a $3.6 billion investment in its Texas plant.

item.title: Trump is attending the NATO summit in Turkey. These are the top issues expected to be discussed
item.description: President Trump arrives in Turkey on Tuesday for a NATO summit, where the focus will be on high-stakes security issues like the Ukraine war. However, the president's decision to wade into the controversy over U.S. striker Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension may cast a shadow. Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: Evidence found behind Cracker Barrel helps solve 1985 cold case murder
item.description: Ohio police say they've solved a man's 1985 murder using old evidence, including items found soon after the killing behind a Cracker Barrel in Georgia.

item.title: Charlie Kirk's alleged killer could face the death penalty if prosecutors' petition succeeds
item.description: The Utah man accused of assassinating right-wing activist Charlie Kirk appeared in court for a preliminary hearing, marking the first time he was in the same room as Kirk's family. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman reports.

item.title: Walmart cuts prices on thousands of items, including beef and soda
item.description: President Trump praised Walmart and said the lower prices are the result of "my Administration's request to celebrate our great Country's 250th birthday."

item.title: Trump travels to Turkey for NATO summit amid global tensions, defense spending needs
item.description: President Trump is expected to continue pressing NATO allies to boost their defense spending as he attends a summit in Turkey. Trump was greeted by Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan as he deplaned. CBS News' Olivia Gazis reports.

item.title: Breaking down U.S. World Cup loss to Belgium and if the red card controversy played a role
item.description: Jimmy Conrad, an analyst for CBS Sports Golazo Network and five-time captain of the U.S. men's national team, joins "CBS Mornings" to break down the U.S. loss to Belgium, if the red card controversy played a role in the match, and who could win it all.

item.title: USMNT eliminated from FIFA World Cup after startling defeat to Belgium, Trump call controversy
item.description: Belgium thrashed the U.S. Men's National Team in a grueling 4-1 defeat, ending their World Cup dreams. CBS Sports Golazo Network's Michael Lahoud reacts to the match.

item.title: Top Democrats call on Platner to drop out as sexual assault allegation emerges
item.description: A Maine woman told Politico that Graham Platner entered her home in late 2021 and forced himself on her. Platner denies the allegation.

item.title: Graham Platner under pressure to drop out of Senate race amid sexual assault allegation
item.description: Party leadership is pressing Maine Democrat Graham Platner to leave the Senate race following sexual assault allegations. CBS News' Fin Gómez explains what could happen if Platner drops out.

item.title: 2,000-year-old gold rings and human bones found by archaeologists
item.description: Archaeologists have discovered eight human skeletons, bronze and gold jewelry and other artifacts indicating a ceremonial burial of wealthy people.

item.title: Here's who won — and lost — under Trump's "big, beautiful bill"
item.description: A year after President Trump signed the sweeping tax and spending package, its effects on households, businesses and federal programs are increasingly evident.

item.title: Woman suspected in bomb attack on Ukrainian magnate found dead in Kyiv
item.description: A woman suspected of planting a bomb that targeted a wealthy Ukrainian businessman in Monaco has been found shot dead in Kyiv.

item.title: Cuba slowly getting power back after third nationwide blackout in 6 months
item.description: Cubans were gradually getting power restored after the third nationwide power outage this year, causing mounting despair in the face of an energy collapse precipitated by a U.S. fuel blockade.

item.title: Ex-Bucknell University coach charged in death of freshman football player
item.description: A former Bucknell University coach has been charged in the death of a freshman football player who collapsed during practice and died in 2024.

item.title: Mom who said her toddlers died after vaccinations charged with murder
item.description: A grand jury indicted Andrea Shaw, who is accused of suffocating her 18-month-old twins, on two counts of first-degree murder.

item.title: Democrats Stevens and El-Sayed face off in primary debate for Michigan Senate
item.description: The debate comes days after a third candidate, Mallory McMorrow, suspended her campaign.

item.title: Senate Democrats push Trump-affiliated companies for answers about IRS settlement
item.description: Democratic Sens. Elizabeth Warren, Chuck Schumer and Ron Wyden are probing whether companies affiliated with the Trump family are included in a DOJ deal to resolve a lawsuit brought by President Trump.

item.title: French President Macron safe after bomb blasts near hotel in Damascus
item.description: France's government says President Emmanuel Macron is safe and will continue his visit to Syria after two bombs exploded outside his hotel in Damascus.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will win war "one way or the other" as Iran holds Khamenei's funeral
item.description: The U.S. is "going to win one way or the other," President Trump said Monday, as Iran held a colossal public funeral for its slain supreme leader.

item.title: FIFA criticized for lifting U.S. star's red card suspension after Trump phone call
item.description: Europe's soccer governing body and prominent commentators have criticized the decision, which allows Folarin Balogun to play against Belgium.

item.title: Belgium knocks out U.S. men 4-1, ending the Americans' World Cup run in Seattle
item.description: Belgium defeated the U.S. Men's National Team​ 4-1 on Monday night in the World Cup round of 16 knockout match in Seattle, ending the Americans' hopes of reaching the quarterfinals for the first time in 24 years.

item.title: Dayslong preliminary hearing for Charlie Kirk's alleged killer is underway
item.description: Prosecutors will present their case this week against Tyler Robinson, the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk.

item.title: 7/6: CBS Evening News
item.description: New Jersey hit with powerful storms after heat wave; passenger recounts seaplane's hard landing in New York City's East River.
